title: Asia Centric: China’s Shoppers Still Buy Luxury, But Selectively
summary: Chinese consumer sentiment isn’t collapsing across the board, but retail sales growth is projected to hit a four-year low. While big-ticket categories like home appliances and autos crumble under subsidy fatigue, highly resilient segments are holding ground, particularly cosmetics, luxury, and premium sportswear. In e-commerce, Alibaba and rival giants are locked in a margin-crushing battle over agentic AI to capture shrinking wallets.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 16
summary: Oil held a three-day gain as the US continued its attacks on Iran in a bid to secure shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Stocks Rise as Inflation Data Outweighs Chip Rout: Markets Wrap
summary: Another report showing softer-than-anticipated inflation drove stocks and bonds higher as Wall Street traders further dialed back wagers on Federal Reserve interest-rate increases.

title: JPMorgan Leads $3 Billion Financing for Warburg’s Pantherx Buy
summary: A group of lenders led by JPMorgan Chase & Co. is lining up a debt package worth up to $3 billion to support Warburg Pincus’ acquisition of Pantherx Specialty LLC, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Emerging Currencies Gain as Traders Cut Back Fed Bets on US PPI
summary: An index tracking emerging-market currencies rose Wednesday as the dollar weakened after softer-than-expected US producer prices drove traders to scale back wagers on Federal Reserve interest-rate increases this year.

title: The Optimistic Case for the Hochul Data Center Pause
summary: All politicians are being forced to navigate this issue.

title: Education Firm Anima Sinks Over 30% as Analysts Pan M&A Deal
summary: Shares of Brazilian education company Anima Holding SA slumped a record 33% on Wednesday after it agreed to repurchase a business it had sold five years ago, with analysts citing high valuation and an increase in leverage.

title: Kalshi Seeks to Let Traders Bet on Flight Cancellation Rates
summary: Your flight? Canceled. The silver lining? You might be able to make some money off the misery.

title: Pool Stocks Sink as Consumers Cautious Despite Scorching Summer
summary: Shares of companies in the swimming pool business sank on Wednesday amid signs that wary consumers are pulling back on spending despite a summer of intense heat.

title: SpaceX Shares Briefly Slip Below IPO Price for First Time
summary: SpaceX shares slumped to their lowest level since the rocket, satellite, and artificial intelligence company went public, briefly falling below their initial public offering price, as investor fanfare quickly evaporated in the month since its trading debut.

title: US Bonds Extend Rally as Soft Inflation Data Dims Fed Hike Bets
summary: Treasuries rose for a second day after soft back-to-back US inflation readings reinforced optimism that US inflation has peaked and may curb the need for the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates.

title: Gold Wavers as Traders Weigh Fed Rate Path After Tame Inflation
summary: Gold wavered as traders weighed the Federal Reserve’s interest-rate path after weaker-than-expected US producer price data eased concerns about an imminent rate hike.

title: Brazil to Offer Billions in Debt Relief for Struggling Farmers
summary: Brazil’s government plans to publish a provisional measure on Wednesday to renegotiate more than 100 billion reais ($20 billion) in rural debt, as President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva prepares to seek reelection in October.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 15
summary: Oil narrowly held onto a three-day winning streak as the US launched fresh strikes on Iran in response to a spate of attacks on shipping vessels and American diesel stockpiles posted a sizeable increase.

title: SpaceX IPO Lifts Wall Street ECM Revenue to Best Since 2021
summary: Wall Street’s biggest investment banks hauled in the most revenue from advising on equity offerings in the second quarter since 2021, fueled by SpaceX’s record-setting IPO and a fundraising blitz for artificial intelligence infrastructure.

title: Short-Sellers Swarm SpaceX With Paper Profit Nearing $4 Billion
summary: SpaceX bears are minting billions on paper as the company’s stock price cracks ahead of a closely-watched rocket launch and upcoming earnings that will unlock a flood of shares.

title: Regional Banks, Wealth Management Lead Financial Services M&A
summary: Smaller deals involving regional banks and wealth managers are driving acquisitions in the financial services industry, according to a clutch of industry watchers.

title: Saudi Gulf Oil Loads Slump as Iran Hits Tankers in Hormuz
summary: Saudi Arabia’s oil loadings from inside the Persian Gulf have slumped in the wake of an upsurge of attacks on supertankers in the Strait of Hormuz, satellite images reviewed by Bloomberg show, demonstrating how Iran is once again disrupting supply.

title: Airbus Set to Win A330 Jet Deals from Flynas, Condor
summary: Airbus SE is in talks to sell more of its A330neo widebody aircraft to Saudi low-cost carrier Flynas Co SJSC and German airline Condor Flugdienst GmbH.

title: Lead Prices Slump as Trafigura Lifts LME Stockpiles to a Record
summary: Lead prices fell toward the lowest since 2022 after Trafigura Group made a second record-breaking delivery of metal into London Metal Exchange warehouses.

title: Russian Fuel Prices Soar as Drone Attacks Curb Refinery Output
summary: Russian gasoline and diesel prices surged further, as continued Ukrainian drone attacks forced the country’s oil refineries to reduce or halt production, deepening a domestic supply crunch.
